retrieved.title: Studying safely
retrieved.text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

Does the main building seem like a maze? Don’t worry: after a guided tour, you’ll find your way through the corridors and stairwells with ease.

**Dates and booking:**

Guided tours will take place from September onwards. The booking link and exact dates will be published here as soon as they are available. The meeting point is at the porter’s desk in the auditorium in the main building of the University of Vienna.

retrieved.title: Our offers and projects
heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
retrieved.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

retrieved.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# The day of the written test

The big day for your degree programme with entrance exam procedure/aptitude test is just around the corner. But what actually happens on a test day? On this page, you will find information about the procedure, permitted and prohibited items, and all the important rules for the day.

The University of Vienna holds the written admission and aptitude tests at the [VIECON – Vienna Congress & Convention Center](https://vieconcenter.at/en/). Please visit the [Wiener Linien website](https://www.wienerlinien.at/web/wl-en) for information on how to get there and tickets for public transport.

You can find the admission time and the expected end of the test on your permission to attend (“Einlassbestätigung”). However, we recommend that you keep the entire day free. Please be punctual and, if necessary, arrive the day before. If you arrive too late, you will be excluded from the entrance exam/aptitude test! There are no alternative dates.

©
Universität Wien

reranked.title: Studying safely
reranked.text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

Does the main building seem like a maze? Don’t worry: after a guided tour, you’ll find your way through the corridors and stairwells with ease.

**Dates and booking:**

Guided tours will take place from September onwards. The booking link and exact dates will be published here as soon as they are available. The meeting point is at the porter’s desk in the auditorium in the main building of the University of Vienna.

Couldn’t get a spot? The [“Uni Wien Guides”](https://event.univie.ac.at/fuehrungen/bereit-fuer-eine-entdeckungsreise/self-guided-tours/app-guides-uni-wien/) app offers a virtual tour with plenty of background information in German and English.

reranked.title: Our offers and projects
heading_path: School meets Uni - Offers for school classes
reranked.text:
The Vienna University Library is open to all high school students free of charge. It supports students with their research for the ABA (formerly VWA) or the BHS diploma thesis. School classes can attend library courses free of charge. The students get to know the university library. They learn how to get a library card and how to borrow books. They are also introduced to research strategies using the central media search engine u:search.

- [Library courses for school classes](https://bibliothek.univie.ac.at/schuelerinnen-schulungen.html)

If you are interested, you can also book a guided tour through the Main Building of the University of Vienna. The guided tour through the Main Building cost €35,-- per school class. A maximum of 30 people per group can participate in the tour. To arrange a tour, please send an email to fuehrungen@univie.ac.at. A combination of guided tour and lecture is the best way to get to know the University of Vienna.

© Universität Wien

reranked.title: Studying safely
reranked.text:
Do not leave your personal belongings unattended in public areas of the University of Vienna.

This also applies to Student Spaces and course rooms.
In the event of theft, you can report it to any police station.

Students spend a lot of time in the digital realm, for example, to participate in courses, to research literature at the library or to interact with fellow students. The following information aims to contribute to your safety online.

© University of Vienna / derknopfdruecker.com

reranked.title: The day of the written test
reranked.text:
[Entrance exam procedure](/en/)

# The day of the written test

The big day for your degree programme with entrance exam procedure/aptitude test is just around the corner. But what actually happens on a test day? On this page, you will find information about the procedure, permitted and prohibited items, and all the important rules for the day.

The University of Vienna holds the written admission and aptitude tests at the [VIECON – Vienna Congress & Convention Center](https://vieconcenter.at/en/). Please visit the [Wiener Linien website](https://www.wienerlinien.at/web/wl-en) for information on how to get there and tickets for public transport.

You can find the admission time and the expected end of the test on your permission to attend (“Einlassbestätigung”). However, we recommend that you keep the entire day free. Please be punctual and, if necessary, arrive the day before. If you arrive too late, you will be excluded from the entrance exam/aptitude test! There are no alternative dates.

©
Universität Wien

selected.title: Studying safely
selected.text:
The University is a large community. Students, academics and admin-istrative staff meet on site and digitally. The following website provides tips and information and the most important contact points for working and studying successfully together.

The emergency text message service notifies you directly via text message in case of a sudden emergency (such as a major fire) at a location of the University of Vienna. It thus helps ensure safe university operations.

Students can register for the emergency text message service on a voluntary basis with their private phone number on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms). You can deregister from the service at any time.

Further information such as about data protection is available on [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms) and in the [Data Protection Declaration of the University of Vienna](https://dsba.univie.ac.at/fileadmin/user_upload/p_dsba/datenschutzerklaerung_websites_V04_26062020_EN.pdf).

[### Register now!](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/web/studium/notfall-sms)

The buildings of the University of Vienna are subject to rules, such as the House Rules or the Laboratory and Workshop Regulations in laboratories. For your own and other people’s safety, information about the procedure in case of fire or accidents is available.

© University of Vienna / Gebhart Sengmüller

selected.title: Welcome at the University of Vienna
selected.text:
Does the main building seem like a maze? Don’t worry: after a guided tour, you’ll find your way through the corridors and stairwells with ease.

**Dates and booking:**

Guided tours will take place from September onwards. The booking link and exact dates will be published here as soon as they are available. The meeting point is at the porter’s desk in the auditorium in the main building of the University of Vienna.

Couldn’t get a spot? The [“Uni Wien Guides”](https://event.univie.ac.at/fuehrungen/bereit-fuer-eine-entdeckungsreise/self-guided-tours/app-guides-uni-wien/) app offers a virtual tour with plenty of background information in German and English.

©
Ian Ehm

selected.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
selected.text: Image 1 of 1 The main building of the University of Vienna houses several institutes, such as the Institute of History, as well as central service facilities, such as the University Library. © University of Vienna / Alex Schuppich The arcade courtyard can be found inside the main building: Here you can learn in good weather or just enjoy the sun. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair The largest lecture hall in the main building of the University of Vienna is the Audimax, which offers more than 750 seats in the hall and on the balcony. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl You can also learn and work well in other locations of the University Library - the Departmental Libraries - for example in the Education, Linguistics and Comparative Literature Library in Sensengasse 3a. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair A location of the University of Vienna near the main building is the NIG - the New Institute Building. Many University Departments are located here like the Department of Cultural and Social Anthropology or of Philosophy. © University of Vienna / Peter Wienerroither Another location of the University of Vienna is the Campus. Here you will find, for example, the Department of Art History or of Romance Studies. With its many green courtyards, the Campus offers space for rest and relaxation in the middle of the city.

selected.title: Why study at the University of Vienna?
selected.text: associated libraries housed. © University of Vienna / Gebhard Sengmueller Similar to the Währinger Straße 29, the Oskar-Morgenstern-Platz 1 has enough learning and communication space for students. © University of Vienna / Barbara Mair Farther away, in Franz-Klein-Gasse 1 in the 19th district, for example the Department of Egyptology and of Classical Archeology are located as well as the Center for Translation Studies. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl At the Center for Translation Studies one can e.g. study the bachelor's program "Transcultural Communication". You can practice the profession of simultaneous interpreter in the language lab. © University of Vienna / Franz Pfluegl In the 15th district one can find the Center for Sports Science and University Sports. prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).
